title: Script R7: Intra and Inter Personal Dissimilarity
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"efqp5","text":"This protocol outlines the analysis used for intra and interpersonal diversity of the virome and whole metagenome using the Bray Curtis dissimilarity metric. We visualize the differences as bar plots and calculate the statistical significance of the differences using a t-test. Based on the methods from the following publication:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"lbbu","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fltva","text":"Hannigan, Geoffrey D., et al. \"The Human Skin Double-Stranded DNA Virome: Topographical and Temporal Diversity, Genetic Enrichment, and Dynamic Associations with the Host Microbiome.\" mBio 6.5 (2015): e01578-15.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":184,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8sm2j","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

locale: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c26ne","text":"## [1] en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8/C/en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"61eop","text":"## ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ej0um","text":"## attached base packages: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"74f83","text":"## [1] stats graphics grDevices utils datasets methods base ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d58vu","text":"## ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ei3lf","text":"## loaded via a namespace (and not attached): ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b5knv","text":"## [1] magrittr_1.5    formatR_1.2    tools_3.2.0    htmltools_0.2.6 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d7246","text":"## [5] yaml_2.1.13    stringi_0.4-1    rmarkdown_0.7    knitr_1.10.5 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2g610","text":"## [9] stringr_1.0.0    digest_0.6.8    evaluate_0.7","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Load the required R packages. library(vegan)packageVersion( library(vegan) packageVersion("vegan") library(ggplot2) packageVersion("ggplot2") library(reshape2) packageVersion("reshape2") library(plyr) packageVersion("plyr") ## [1] '2.3.0' ## [1] '1.0.1' ## [1] '1.4.1' ## [1] '1.8.2'

Load in the OTU table (here they are for the virome samples) INPUT INPUT <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Interpersonal_intrapersonal_dissimilarity/contig_otu_table_transposed_formatted.txt", header=TRUE, sep="\t") head(INPUT)[,c(1:6)] %s %s ## ContigID X1 X2 X3 X4 X5 ## 1 MG100098 11.91350 0.00000 0 0 28.7690 ## 2 MG100099 28.72340 0.00000 0 0 0.0000 ## 3 MG100100 73.04680 5.90828 0 0 34.0190 ## 4 MG100101 4.18674 0.00000 0 0 0.0000 ## 5 MG100102 22.93320 4.99401 0 0 0.0000 ## 6 MG100103 13.01880 9.21378 0 0 17.6838
Remove last column because it contains NAs (artifact of the python script used to transpose this file.) INPUT_NO_FINAL

Input mapping file. INPUT_MAP INPUT_MAP <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Mapping_files/SkinMet_and_Virome_001_metadata.tsv", header=TRUE) head(INPUT_MAP)[,c(1:6)] %s %s ## NexteraXT_SampleID NexteraXT_RunName NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID ## 1 MG100151 NexteraXT_007 MG100102 ## 2 MG100150 NexteraXT_007 MG100101 ## 3 MG100149 NexteraXT_007 <NA> ## 4 MG100146 NexteraXT_007 MG100098 ## 5 MG100157 NexteraXT_007 MG100107 ## 6 MG100153 NexteraXT_007 MG100104 ## NexteraXT_Virome_RunName SubjectID TimePoint ## 1 NexteraXT_005 1 1 ## 2 NexteraXT_005 1 1 ## 3 <NA> 1 1 ## 4 NexteraXT_005 1 1 ## 5 NexteraXT_005 1 1 ## 6 NexteraXT_005 1 1
Now we need to format the data and generate a dissimilarity matrix using the Bray-Curtis metric. We will use this matrix to extract the dissimilarity values between our samples below.

Generate subset of mapping file for only the specific anatomic sites and all time points (2 and 3). SUBSET_MAP SUBSET_MAP <- INPUT_MAP[-which(INPUT_MAP$NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID %in% NA), ] SUBSET_MAP <- SUBSET_MAP[which(SUBSET_MAP$TimePoint %in% c(2,3)), ] SUBSET_MAP <- SUBSET_MAP[-which(SUBSET_MAP$Site_Symbol %in% c("Ba","Ph","Vf","Neg")), ] SUBSET_MAP <- SUBSET_MAP[-which(SUBSET_MAP$SubjectID %in% c(2,3,9,11)), ]

Get only the samples described in the map subset. KEEP_SAMPLES KEEP_SAMPLES <- as.vector(SUBSET_MAP$NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID) INPUT_SUBSET <- INPUT_NO_FINAL[which(INPUT_NO_FINAL$ContigID %in% c(KEEP_SAMPLES)), ] row.names(INPUT_SUBSET) <- INPUT_SUBSET[,1] INPUT_SUB_FORMAT <- INPUT_SUBSET[,-1] head(INPUT_SUB_FORMAT)[,c(1:6)] %s %s ## X1 X2 X3 X4 X5 X6 ## 1 MG100195 0.0000 0 0.508673 1.343610 2.71473 0 ## 2 MG100198 0.0000 0 0.000000 0.363769 0.00000 0 ## 3 MG100199 0.0000 0 0.059621 0.955394 0.00000 0 ## 4 MG100200 25.9488 0 0.618683 0.457572 2.31129 0 ## 5 MG100201 0.0000 0 0.126176 0.155531 0.00000 0 ## 6 MG100202 50.3225 0 0.552366 1.361750 5.15885 0
Generate distance matrix using Bray Curtis for all time points (2 and 3). INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX

We need to obtain a data frame with all of the distance information between specific combinations of sample distances. Intrapersonal dissimilarities will be between the same location and subject, but at time point 2 vs 3. Interpersonal distance will be between the sample and any other given sample of that time point.
Get intrapersonal and interpersonal distance similarities, showing intra over time is more similar than that site compared to all other sites. INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX_MATRIX

Data frame reference: "sample tp2" \t "sample tp3". Using merge function. MAP_TP2 MAP_TP2 <- SUBSET_MAP[c(SUBSET_MAP$TimePoint==2), ] MAP_TP3 <- SUBSET_MAP[c(SUBSET_MAP$TimePoint==3), ] MAP_MERGE_REF <- merge(MAP_TP2, MAP_TP3, by=c("SubjectID","Site_Symbol")) SAMPLE_NAMES <- as.vector(MAP_MERGE_REF$NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID.x) INTRAPERSONAL_DIST <- data.frame(lapply(SAMPLE_NAMES, function(i) { INTRAPERSON_DIST <- INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX_MATRIX[c(row.names(INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX_MATRIX)==i), as.vector(MAP_MERGE_REF[MAP_MERGE_REF$NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID.x==i,"NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID.y"])] SUBJECT <- MAP_MERGE_REF[MAP_MERGE_REF$NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID.x==i,"SubjectID"] SITE <- as.vector(MAP_MERGE_REF[MAP_MERGE_REF$NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID.x==i,"Site_Symbol"]) RESULT <- data.frame(X=c(i, SUBJECT, SITE, INTRAPERSON_DIST)) return(RESULT) })) head(INTRAPERSONAL_DIST)[,c(1:4)] INTERPERSONAL_DIST_TP3 <- data.frame(lapply(SAMPLE_NAMES, function(i) { INTERPERSON_DIST_TP3 <- INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX_MATRIX[c(row.names(INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX_MATRIX)==i), as.vector(MAP_MERGE_REF[-which(MAP_MERGE_REF$NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID.x %in% i),"NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID.y"])] SUBJECT <- MAP_MERGE_REF[MAP_MERGE_REF$NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID.x==i,"SubjectID"] SITE <- as.vector(MAP_MERGE_REF[MAP_MERGE_REF$NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID.x==i,"Site_Symbol"]) TRANS <- data.frame(t(INTERPERSON_DIST_TP3)) RESULT <- data.frame(X=c(SUBJECT, SITE, INTERPERSON_DIST_TP3)) return(TRANS) })) head(INTERPERSONAL_DIST_TP3)[,c(1:4)] INTERPERSONAL_DIST_TP2 <- data.frame(lapply(SAMPLE_NAMES, function(i) { INTERPERSON_DIST_T2 <- INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX_MATRIX[c(row.names(INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX_MATRIX)==i), as.vector(MAP_MERGE_REF[-which(MAP_MERGE_REF$NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID.x %in% i),"NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID.x"])] SUBJECT <- MAP_MERGE_REF[MAP_MERGE_REF$NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID.x==i,"SubjectID"] SITE <- as.vector(MAP_MERGE_REF[MAP_MERGE_REF$NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID.x==i,"Site_Symbol"]) TRANS <- data.frame(t(INTERPERSON_DIST_T2)) RESULT <- data.frame(X=c(SUBJECT, SITE, INTERPERSON_DIST_T2)) return(TRANS) })) head(INTERPERSONAL_DIST_TP2)[,c(1:4)] %s %s ## X X.1 X.2 X.3 ## 1 MG100425 MG100283 MG100251 MG100267 ## 2 1 1 1 1 ## 3 Ac Ax Fh Oc ## 4 0.68106733910108 0.627045291391653 0.38613104099571 0.606457024500615 %s %s ## MG100425 MG100283 MG100251 MG100267 ## MG100632 0.8905969 0.7000370 0.6604504 0.6138396 ## MG100457 0.5052325 0.7546691 0.8898742 0.8971785 ## MG100616 0.7112431 0.7428203 0.6193057 0.3955118 ## MG100647 0.7544944 0.7635985 0.7276292 0.7268521 ## MG100441 0.6827725 0.8227614 0.6277412 0.5954542 ## MG100473 0.5301470 0.7833859 0.4859976 0.5672776 %s %s ## MG100425 MG100283 MG100251 MG100267 ## MG100283 0.7811457 0.7811457 0.4822732 0.5395027 ## MG100251 0.4822732 0.7636557 0.7636557 0.7703925 ## MG100267 0.5395027 0.7703925 0.3816939 0.3816939 ## MG100299 0.8537536 0.8952908 0.7917489 0.7957845 ## MG100195 0.8723981 0.9160835 0.8147091 0.8071813 ## MG100235 0.5958880 0.7292550 0.4572400 0.5011740

Melt the two interpersonal distance data frames. INTER_TP2_MELT INTER_TP2_MELT <- melt(INTERPERSONAL_DIST_TP2) INTER_TP2_MELT$Type <- "Inter" INTER_TP3_MELT <- melt(INTERPERSONAL_DIST_TP3) ## No id variables; using all as measure variables

Get intrapersonal values in same format. INTER_TP3_MELT$Type INTER_TP3_MELT$Type <- "Inter" INTRA_TRANS <- data.frame(t(INTRAPERSONAL_DIST)) INTRA_TRANS_CUT <- INTRA_TRANS[,c("X1","X4")] INTRA_TRANS_CUT$Type <- "Intra" colnames(INTRA_TRANS_CUT) <- c("variable","value","Type") INTRA_TRANS_CUT$value <- as.numeric(as.character(INTRA_TRANS_CUT$value)) row.names(INTRA_TRANS_CUT) <- NULL

Bind together all of these data frames. BOUND_DIST BOUND_DIST <- rbind(INTRA_TRANS_CUT, INTER_TP2_MELT, INTER_TP3_MELT) BOUND_DIST <- BOUND_DIST[,c(2,3)] head(BOUND_DIST) %s %s ## value Type ## 1 0.6810673 Intra ## 2 0.6270453 Intra ## 3 0.3861310 Intra ## 4 0.6064570 Intra ## 5 0.8635676 Intra ## 6 0.8629480 Intra

Plot the resulting distances as means with standard error. BOUND_SUMMARY BOUND_SUMMARY <- ddply(BOUND_DIST, c("Type"), summarise, N=length(value), mean=mean(value), sd=sd(value), se=sd/sqrt(N)) head(BOUND_SUMMARY) %s %s ## Type N mean sd se ## 1 Inter 30504 0.7217809 0.1400811 0.0008020493 ## 2 Intra 124 0.6310340 0.1350353 0.0121265309
Visualize the data as a nice blue bar plot. ggplot(BOUND_SUMMARY, aes(x=Type, y=mean)) + theme_bw() + geom_bar(position=position_dodge(), stat=

Perform a t-test to determine the statistical significance of the difference between the two populations. t.test(BOUND_DIST$value ~ BOUND_DIST$Type) ## ## Welch Two Sample t-test ## ## data: BOUND_DIST$value by BOUND_DIST$Type ## t = 7.467, df = 124.08, p-value = 1.264e-11 ## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means is not equal to 0 ## 95 percent confidence interval: ## 0.0666928 0.1148010 ## sample estimates: ## mean in group Inter mean in group Intra ## 0.7217809 0.6310340

Finally, look at the temporal variation using the Jaccard Similarity Index. BOUND_DIST$simlr BOUND_DIST$simlr <- 1- BOUND_DIST$value BOUND_DIST_SUB <- BOUND_DIST[c(which(BOUND_DIST$Type %in% "Intra")),] JaccardPlot <- ggplot(BOUND_DIST_SUB, aes(x=Type, y=simlr)) + theme_classic() + geom_jitter(position = position_jitter(width = .2)) JaccardPlot
creator: Geoffrey D Hannigan
